title: Wall Street Journal: California-Born 'El 03' Consolidating Control of CJNG Following El Mencho's Death
detail: The Wall Street Journal published a detailed report confirming that Juan Carlos Valencia González, alias 'El 03,' a 41-year-old California-born U.S. citizen and stepson of the late El Mencho, has emerged as the de facto leader of CJNG following Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes's killing on February 22. The U.S. Department of State and the National Counterterrorism Center (NCTC) have both identified Valencia González as the cartel's new primary leader. He previously commanded Grupo Élite, the CJNG's militarized armed wing operating in Michoacán, Zacatecas, and Guanajuato. The U.S. government is offering a $5 million reward for information leading to his capture. Security experts note that his U.S. citizenship creates unprecedented legal complications for counter-cartel operations, while also raising questions about his ability to sustain internal cartel loyalty without El Mencho's founding authority.

media.caption: Wall Street Journal confirms California-born El 03 consolidating control of CJNG

title: CJNG Exploits Sinaloa Cartel Fracture, Advances Into Contested Border Territories
detail: ACLED conflict tracking data and InSight Crime analysis confirm that CJNG has accelerated territorial expansion along several fronts, exploiting the continued internal fracture of the Sinaloa Cartel following the July 2024 arrest of Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada. CJNG fighters have launched offensives along the border of Sinaloa state, while also intensifying operations in Baja California, Zacatecas, and Chiapas. Los Chapitos — one of the warring Sinaloa factions — reportedly announced a tactical alliance with CJNG to counter El Mayo's faction. Security analysts warn that the combination of CJNG's succession crisis and the Sinaloa civil war is creating an unprecedented reorganization of Mexico's criminal landscape, with violence potentially spiking in previously stable corridors.

media.caption: CJNG exploits Sinaloa Cartel fracture, advances into contested border territories
